name: nostr-bray
description: Trust-aware Nostr for AI and humans. Three dimensions of trust -- verification, proximity, and access -- woven into every interaction.
note: Parameter schemas for every registered tool. Most tools live in the catalog (discoverable via search-actions + execute-action); ~48 promoted tools are exposed directly on the MCP server. This manifest covers both.

tools.name: article-list
tools.description: List long-form article metadata (NIP-23, kind 30023) by author — titles, slugs, summaries, and dates without full content. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

author.description: Author — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: article-publish
tools.description: Publish a long-form article (NIP-23, kind 30023). Creates a replaceable event with title, markdown content, and optional metadata. The slug (d-tag) defaults to a slugified version of the title.

published_at.description: Publication date as ISO string (defaults to now)

slug.description: URL-safe identifier (d-tag) — defaults to slugified title

tools.name: article-read
tools.description: Read long-form article(s) (NIP-23, kind 30023) by author. Optionally fetch a specific article by slug. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

author.description: Author — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

slug.description: Article slug (d-tag) — omit to fetch all articles by author

tools.name: badge-accept
tools.description: Accept a badge and add it to your profile badges (NIP-58, kind 30008). Updates your profile badge list preserving existing badges.

award_event_id.description: Event ID of the badge award (kind 8)

tools.name: badge-award
tools.description: Award a badge to one or more recipients (NIP-58, kind 8). The badge must be defined first via badge-create.

recipients.description: Recipients — names, NIP-05, npubs, or hex pubkeys

tools.name: badge-create
tools.description: Define a new badge (NIP-58, kind 30009). Creates a replaceable badge definition with name, description, and optional image.

tools.name: badge-list
tools.description: List badges defined by an author or displayed on a profile (NIP-58). Mode "defined" shows badge definitions, "profile" shows accepted badges.

pubkey.description: Identity — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

mode.description: "defined" for badges created by this person, "profile" for badges they display

tools.name: blossom-check
tools.description: Verify a blob exists and is intact on a blossom server. HEAD request to check existence, optionally downloads and verifies SHA-256 hash matches.

sha256.description: Expected SHA-256 hash of the blob

verify.description: Download and verify hash integrity (slower but thorough)

tools.name: blossom-delete
tools.description: Delete a blob from a blossom media server by SHA-256 hash.

tools.name: blossom-discover
tools.description: Discover blossom servers used by contacts. Fetches kind 10063 (NIP-B7 server list) events from the given pubkeys and aggregates unique server URLs. Use contacts-get first to get pubkeys.

pubkeys.description: Hex pubkeys to discover servers from (e.g. your contacts)

tools.name: blossom-list
tools.description: List blobs uploaded by a pubkey on a blossom server.

pubkeyHex.description: Hex pubkey to list blobs for

tools.name: blossom-mirror
tools.description: Upload a file to multiple blossom servers for redundancy. Provide a source URL (existing blob), file path, or data. Uploads to each server in parallel and returns per-server results with verified SHA-256 hash.

servers.description: Target blossom server URLs to mirror to

sourceUrl.description: URL of an existing blob to mirror (fetched and re-uploaded)

filePath.description: Path to a local file to mirror

tools.name: blossom-repair
tools.description: Find a broken blob on other blossom servers by SHA-256 hash and optionally re-upload to a target server. Searches each server, verifies hash integrity, and re-uploads if found.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hash of the missing/broken blob

searchServers.description: Blossom servers to search for the blob

targetServer.description: Server to re-upload to if found (omit to just locate)

tools.name: blossom-servers
tools.description: Read or update your preferred blossom server list (kind 10063). Without servers parameter, reads the current list. With servers parameter, publishes a new list.

servers.description: New server list to publish (omit to read current list)

tools.name: blossom-upload
tools.description: Upload a file to a blossom media server. Returns the blob URL and SHA-256 hash.

filePath.description: Path to the file to upload

tools.name: blossom-usage
tools.description: Check storage usage across blossom servers for a pubkey. Returns per-server blob count and total size, plus aggregate totals.

pubkeyHex.description: Hex pubkey to check usage for

tools.name: blossom-verify
tools.description: Verify all media URLs in a note are still accessible. Pass the note content and get back alive/broken status per URL. Optionally verifies SHA-256 hash integrity for blossom URLs.

content.description: Note content containing media URLs to verify

verifyHash.description: Also verify SHA-256 hashes for blossom URLs (slower)

tools.name: calendar-create
tools.description: Create a calendar event (NIP-52). Auto-detects date-based (kind 31922, YYYY-MM-DD) vs time-based (kind 31923, ISO datetime). Returns the signed event and publish result.

start.description: Start — YYYY-MM-DD for date-based, ISO datetime for time-based (auto-detected)

participants.description: Participant identifiers — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

slug.description: URL-safe identifier (d-tag) — defaults to slugified title

tools.name: calendar-read
tools.description: Fetch calendar events (NIP-52, kinds 31922 + 31923) by author and/or date range. Accepts any identifier for author: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

author.description: Author — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

since.description: Only events starting after this date (ISO datetime or YYYY-MM-DD)

until.description: Only events starting before this date (ISO datetime or YYYY-MM-DD)

tools.name: calendar-rsvp
tools.description: RSVP to a calendar event (NIP-52, kind 31925). Pass the event coordinate (kind:pubkey:d-tag format) and your status.

tools.name: canary-beacon-check
tools.description: Check a CANARY beacon's status. Pass a beaconId to check a locally tracked beacon, or pass groupId + encrypted content to decrypt and check an incoming beacon. Returns status: "alive" (within expected interval), "overdue" (missed 2+ intervals).

tools.name: canary-beacon-create
tools.description: Create an encrypted liveness beacon for a CANARY group. Encrypts the location with the group's beacon key (AES-256-GCM). Returns encrypted content suitable for publishing as a Nostr event.

tools.name: canary-duress-detect
tools.description: Check if a spoken verification word is a duress signal. Returns whether the word matches a member's duress token and which member(s) signalled.

spokenWord.description: The word to check for duress

tools.name: canary-duress-signal
tools.description: Generate a duress signal for a CANARY group. Returns the duress word (indistinguishable from a normal verification word to anyone without the group secret) and an encrypted alert payload. Speaking the duress word silently triggers the alarm for other group members.

geohash.description: Current location geohash (for the duress alert)

tools.name: canary-group-create
tools.description: Create an encrypted CANARY verification group. All members share a secret seed that generates time-rotating verification words. Any member can verify any other. Presets: "family" (weekly, casual), "field-ops" (daily, high-security), "enterprise" (48h, corporate), "event" (4h, conferences). The active identity is automatically added as creator and admin.

members.description: Member pubkeys (64-char hex). Creator is auto-added if missing.

rotationInterval.description: Custom rotation interval in seconds (overrides preset)

tools.name: canary-group-current
tools.description: Get the current verification word for a CANARY group. All group members derive the same word from the shared seed and current time window.

groupId.description: Group ID from canary-group-create or canary-group-join

tools.name: canary-group-join
tools.description: Join an existing CANARY group using a shared seed (received via NIP-17 DM). Reconstructs the group state locally so you can verify words and beacons.

seed.description: Group seed (64-char hex, received from group creator)

tools.name: canary-group-members
tools.description: List members and admins of a CANARY group.

tools.name: canary-group-verify
tools.description: Verify a spoken word against a CANARY group. Returns "verified" (exact match), "duress" (coercion signal from a specific member), "stale" (valid but from an adjacent time window), or "failed" (no match).

spokenWord.description: The word spoken by a group member

tools.name: canary-session-create
tools.description: Create a CANARY verification session for two-party spoken verification. Uses the active identity's key as the shared secret. Presets: "call" (phone verification, 30s rotation) or "handoff" (physical handoff, single-use). Returns the session ID and the word you should speak to prove your identity.

roles.description: The two roles in the session (e.g. ["caller", "agent"])

rotationSeconds.description: Custom rotation interval in seconds (overrides preset)

theirIdentity.description: Other party's identity string for duress detection

counter.description: Fixed counter for handoff mode (required when rotationSeconds=0)

tools.name: canary-session-current
tools.description: Get the current verification words for an active CANARY session. Returns your token (what you speak) and their token (what you expect to hear).

tools.name: canary-session-verify
tools.description: Verify a spoken word against an active CANARY session. Returns status: "valid" (correct word), "duress" (coercion signal detected), or "invalid" (wrong word).

spokenWord.description: The word spoken by the other party

tools.name: cast-spell
tools.description: Execute a NIP-A7 Spell (kind 777). Fetches the Spell event, resolves runtime variables ($me → your pubkey, $contacts → your follow list) and relative timestamps (e.g. "7d", "30d"), builds a REQ filter, and queries relays. Pass either an event ID or a Spell event object from a prior relay-query.

eventId.description: Event ID (hex) of the kind 777 Spell to cast

relays.description: Override relays for the result query

tools.name: community-approve
tools.description: Approve a post in a community you moderate (NIP-72, kind 4550). Wraps the original event for inclusion in the community feed.

event_id.description: Event ID of the post to approve

event_pubkey.description: Author of the post — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: community-create
tools.description: Create a moderated community (NIP-72, kind 34550). Communities are open and approval-based, different from NIP-29 closed groups.

moderators.description: Moderator identities — names, NIP-05, npubs, or hex pubkeys

tools.name: community-feed
tools.description: Read approved posts in a community (NIP-72). Fetches kind 4550 approved posts and unwraps the original content.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only posts after this time

tools.name: community-list
tools.description: Discover communities on Nostr (NIP-72). Fetches kind 34550 community definitions.

tools.name: community-post
tools.description: Post to a community (NIP-72). Creates a kind 1 note with an a-tag pointing to the community. A moderator must approve it before it appears in the feed.

tools.name: contacts-follow
tools.description: Follow a Nostr pubkey. Fetches current contact list, adds the pubkey, publishes updated kind 3. If the list would shrink by >20%, returns a guarded warning — pass confirm: true to proceed.

confirm.description: Set true to bypass the contacts safety guard

tools.name: contacts-get
tools.description: Fetch the contact list (kind 3 follows) for a Nostr identity. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

pubkey.description: Identity to fetch contacts for — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: contacts-search
tools.description: Search your contacts by name, display name, or NIP-05. Resolves profiles in one batch — much faster than fetching each individually. Use this to find a contact when you know their name but not their pubkey.

query.description: Search string (matches name, display_name, nip05, petname — case insensitive)

pubkeyHex.description: Hex pubkey whose contacts to search (defaults to active identity)

npub.description: Bech32 npub for relay routing (defaults to active identity)

tools.name: contacts-unfollow
tools.description: Unfollow a Nostr pubkey. Fetches current contact list, removes the pubkey, publishes updated kind 3. If the list would shrink by >20%, returns a guarded warning — pass confirm: true to proceed.

confirm.description: Set true to bypass the contacts safety guard

tools.name: count
tools.description: Count events matching a filter on the active identity's relays. Filter fields may be supplied either as top-level arguments or wrapped in a single "filter" object (both shapes are accepted).

limit.description: Maximum number of events to count

filter.description: Alias: all filter fields wrapped in a single object. Merged with any top-level fields; top-level values win on conflict.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to count

tools.name: decode
tools.description: Decode a nip19 entity (npub, nsec, note, nevent, nprofile, naddr) or nostr: URI to its components.

input.description: nip19 string or nostr: URI to decode

tools.name: dispatch-ack
tools.description: Acknowledge receipt of a dispatch task. Tells the sender "got it, working on it" before you have a full result. Use when a task will take time to complete.

re.description: Task ID being acknowledged (the "id" field from dispatch-check output)

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

note.description: Optional status note (e.g. "starting analysis of toll-booth")

tools.name: dispatch-cancel
tools.description: Cancel a dispatch task you previously sent or received. Notifies the other party that the task should be abandoned.

re.description: Task ID to cancel (the "id" field from the dispatch message)

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: dispatch-capability-discover
tools.description: Discover dispatch-capable agents on Nostr. Searches for NIP-89 capability cards (kind 31990) tagged with the dispatch protocol. Optionally filter by task type. Returns agent names, descriptions, supported task types, repos, and availability.

tools.name: dispatch-capability-publish
tools.description: Publish your agent's dispatch capabilities as a NIP-89 capability card (kind 31990). Lets other agents discover you on Nostr without needing a pre-shared identities file. The card advertises what task types you support, which repos you can work on, and your availability.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Full-stack TypeScript agent for TROTT ecosystem")

task_types.description: Supported task types: "think", "build", or custom types

repos.description: Repository names this agent can work on (e.g. ["toll-booth", "trott-sdk"])

max_depth.description: Maximum delegation depth this agent accepts

slug.description: Unique d-tag identifier (defaults to slugified name)

tools.name: dispatch-capability-read
tools.description: Read a specific agent's dispatch capability card. Fetches their NIP-89 kind 31990 event to see what task types they support, which repos they work on, and their current availability.

pubkey.description: Agent pubkey — name ("alice"), NIP-05 ("alice@example.com"), npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: dispatch-check
tools.description: Check for incoming collaboration tasks from trusted Nostr identities. Returns dispatch messages: think tasks, build tasks, results, acks, status updates. Only shows messages from identities in the dispatch trust list, validated for freshness. Defaults to messages received since this session started (prevents re-processing old tasks). Pass since=0 to see all available messages regardless of age.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only return messages after this time. Defaults to session start (prevents duplicate processing). Pass 0 to fetch all available messages.

tools.name: dispatch-failure
tools.description: Report that a dispatch task failed after you attempted it. Use when you tried to complete the task but encountered errors. Optionally include partial results so work is not lost.

re.description: Task ID that failed (the "id" field from dispatch-check output)

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

error.description: What went wrong (e.g. "build failed: type error in handlers.ts:42")

partial.description: Any partial results or progress made before the failure

tools.name: dispatch-propose
tools.description: Propose an alternative approach for a dispatch task. Use when you can't do exactly what was asked but have a better idea. The sender receives the proposal via dispatch-check.

re.description: Task ID you are proposing an alternative for

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

proposal.description: What you propose to do instead

reason.description: Why the original approach won't work

tools.name: dispatch-query
tools.description: Ask the task sender a clarifying question before delivering results. Use mid-task when you need more information to proceed. The sender receives the question via dispatch-check and can reply.

re.description: Task ID you are asking about (the "id" field from dispatch-check output)

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: dispatch-refuse
tools.description: Refuse a dispatch task you cannot or should not complete. Tells the sender "I cannot do this" with a reason. Use when the task is outside your capabilities, scope, or permissions.

re.description: Task ID being refused (the "id" field from dispatch-check output)

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

reason.description: Why you are refusing (e.g. "repo not available", "task requires write access I do not have")

tools.name: dispatch-reply
tools.description: Send a result back for a completed dispatch task. Use after finishing a think or build task received via dispatch-check. Only replies to trusted identities. Do not use this for human conversations — only for responding to tasks from other AI agents.

re.description: Task ID from the received message (the "id" field from dispatch-check output)

to.description: Recipient — name ("alice"), NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey. Use the sender from the original dispatch message.

type.description: Whether this is a think (analysis) or build (implementation) result

plan.description: Analysis text — summaries and recommendations, NOT verbatim file contents

branch.description: Git branch created for build results

delete_event_id.description: Event ID to delete after reply (use the eventId from dispatch-check for NIP-09 cleanup)

tools.name: dispatch-send
tools.description: Send a collaboration task to a trusted Nostr identity. Think tasks request read-only code analysis. Build tasks request implementation with code changes. The recipient's AI agent receives the task via encrypted NIP-17 DM and processes it with dispatch-check. Uses the active identity as sender — switch identity first if needed.

to.description: Recipient — name ("alice"), NIP-05 ("alice@example.com"), npub, or hex pubkey

type.description: "think" for read-only analysis (questions, reviews), "build" for implementation (code changes, PRs)

prompt.description: The task description. For think: what to analyse and what questions to answer. For build: what to implement and acceptance criteria.

repos.description: Repository directory names the recipient needs (e.g. ["toll-booth", "trott-sdk"])

context_id.description: Conversation/session ID for grouping related tasks. Pass the same ID across a multi-turn exchange.

depth.description: Delegation depth limit. Decremented when forwarding tasks. Prevents infinite delegation chains. Default: no limit.

depends_on.description: Task IDs that must complete before the recipient should start this task. The recipient should check results for these IDs before proceeding.

tools.name: dispatch-status
tools.description: Send a status update to collaborators. Use to broadcast availability (e.g. "busy until 14:00"), queue depth, or progress on long-running tasks.

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

resets_at.description: ISO timestamp when status resets (e.g. when you will be available again)

tools.name: dm-by-name
tools.description: Send an encrypted DM to a contact by name — no pubkey needed. Searches your contacts, finds the match, and sends the message. Returns an error if zero or multiple matches are found (use contacts-search to disambiguate).

name.description: Contact name to search for (matches name, display_name, nip05 — case insensitive)

nip04.description: Use legacy NIP-04 instead of NIP-17

tools.name: dm-conversation
tools.description: Read DM conversation with a specific person. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey. Shows messages in chronological order.

with.description: Person to show conversation with — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

limit.description: Max messages to fetch before filtering

tools.name: dm-read
tools.description: Read direct messages addressed to the active identity. Decrypts both NIP-17 (gift wrap) and NIP-04 (legacy). Each message includes { from, content, protocol, decrypted }. Gracefully handles decryption failures without crashing.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only return DMs after this time

tools.name: dm-send
tools.description: Send an encrypted direct message. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey. Default: NIP-17 gift wrap (most private). Set nip04: true for legacy NIP-04 (only if NIP04_ENABLED=1). Use dm-by-name to search your contacts if you are unsure of the exact identity.

to.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05 ("user@domain"), npub, nprofile, or hex pubkey

nip04.description: Use legacy NIP-04 instead of NIP-17

tools.name: encode-naddr
tools.description: Encode an addressable event reference as a bech32 naddr.

tools.name: encode-nevent
tools.description: Encode an event ID with relay hints and optional author as a bech32 nevent.

tools.name: encode-note
tools.description: Encode a hex event ID as a bech32 note.

tools.name: encode-nprofile
tools.description: Encode a hex pubkey with relay hints as a bech32 nprofile.

tools.name: encode-npub
tools.description: Encode a hex public key as a bech32 npub.

tools.name: encode-nsec
tools.description: Encode a hex private key as a bech32 nsec. WARNING: private key material flows through the MCP transport.

tools.name: feed-by-name
tools.description: Fetch recent posts by a contact, searching by name instead of pubkey. Returns an error if zero or multiple contacts match.

name.description: Contact name to search for (case insensitive)

since.description: Unix timestamp — only fetch posts after this time

tools.name: feed-discover
tools.description: Discover new accounts worth following, ranked by trust score. Strategies: trust-adjacent (contacts-of-contacts), topic (hashtag search), active (recent posters in your network).

query.description: Topic or hashtag for topic strategy

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch events by nip19 code (note, nevent, nprofile, npub, naddr). Resolves the entity and queries relays.

tools.name: filter
tools.description: Test if a Nostr event matches a given filter. Returns true or false.

tools.name: group-add-user
tools.description: Add or update a user's membership in a NIP-29 group (kind 9000 admin event). Optionally assign a role.

tools.name: group-chat
tools.description: Fetch recent chat messages from a NIP-29 group.

tools.name: group-create
tools.description: Create a NIP-29 group (kind 9004 admin event). The relay assigns membership state from the event. Optionally supply a group ID; the relay may derive its own from the event ID.

isOpen.description: True = open (anyone can join); false = closed (invite-only)

tools.name: group-info
tools.description: Fetch group metadata (name, about, picture) for a NIP-29 group.

tools.name: group-members
tools.description: List members of a NIP-29 group.

tools.name: group-remove-user
tools.description: Remove a user from a NIP-29 group (kind 9001 admin event).

tools.name: group-send
tools.description: Send a message to a NIP-29 group.

tools.name: group-set-roles
tools.description: Define role names and their permissions in a NIP-29 group (kind 9007 admin event). Each role entry is { name, permissions? }.

tools.name: group-update
tools.description: Update NIP-29 group metadata (kind 9002 admin event). Supply only the fields you want to change.

tools.name: handler-discover
tools.description: Discover MCP-capable handlers on Nostr. Searches for kind 31990 events that include mcp transport tags. Optionally filter by supported event kind. Returns handler names, descriptions, supported kinds, and transport endpoints.

tools.name: handler-publish
tools.description: Publish a NIP-XX Machine Application Handler card (kind 31990) advertising MCP transport endpoints. Lets other agents and tools discover MCP servers on Nostr by the event kinds they support. Provide at least one of stdio_command or http_url.

d_tag.description: Custom d-tag identifier (defaults to slugified name)

tools.name: hashtag-feed
tools.description: Fetch notes (kind 1) with a specific hashtag. Uses standard tag filtering — works on all relays, no NIP-50 required.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only notes after this time

tools.name: identity-accept-migration
tools.description: Accept a migration from an external signer (Amber, nsec.app, etc). Signs and publishes the acceptance event via the current signer. Optionally verifies the migration event from the old key. Requires a Heartwood-compatible signer or local key.

oldNpub.description: Bech32 npub of the identity migrating to this signer

migrationEventId.description: Event ID of the migration event signed by the old key (for verification)

tools.name: identity-backup
tools.description: Fetch profile, contacts, relay list, and attestations for a pubkey. Returns a portable JSON bundle (no private keys).

npub.description: Bech32 npub for relay routing (defaults to active identity)

tools.name: identity-backup-shamir
tools.description: Split the active identity's private key into Shamir shard files using threshold secret sharing. Any "threshold" shards can reconstruct the key. Files written with 0600 permissions via atomic rename. Returns file paths only — shard content never appears in the response.

shares.description: Total number of shards to create

outputDir.description: Directory to write shard files to

tools.name: identity-create
tools.description: Generate a fresh Nostr identity with a BIP-39 mnemonic seed. Returns { npub, mnemonic }. Store the mnemonic securely — it will not be shown again. Use this when the user needs a brand new identity unrelated to the current one.

tools.name: identity-derive
tools.description: Derive a child Nostr identity from the master key by purpose and index. Deterministic — same inputs always produce the same npub. Returns { npub, purpose, index }. Use identity_switch after deriving to operate as the new identity.

tools.name: identity-derive-persona
tools.description: Derive a named persona (e.g. "work", "personal", "anonymous"). Like identity_derive but uses a human-readable name. Returns { npub, personaName, index }. Follow with identity_switch to activate.

tools.name: identity-list
tools.description: List all known identities (master + all derived). Returns array of { npub, purpose, index, personaName }. Never includes private keys. Use this to see what identities are available before switching.

tools.name: identity-migrate
tools.description: Migrate from an old identity to the active one. Shows preview first — set confirm: true to execute. Publishes linkage proof and re-signs migratable events.

oldPubkeyHex.description: Hex pubkey of the old identity

oldNpub.description: Bech32 npub of the old identity

confirm.description: Set true to execute migration (preview by default)

tools.name: identity-prove
tools.description: Create a cryptographic linkage proof between the master key and the active identity. "blind" (default) proves they share a master without revealing how the child was derived. "full" also reveals the purpose and index. Returns a LinkageProof object that can be verified by anyone with nsec-tree. Use trust_proof_publish to make it permanent on relays.

mode.description: Proof mode: "blind" hides derivation path, "full" reveals purpose and index

tools.name: identity-recover
tools.description: Recover a master identity from Shamir word-list shard files. Reconstructs the secret, derives the master npub, and optionally configures new relays. This operation is destructive — use only when the original identity is inaccessible.

shardPaths.description: Absolute file paths to Shamir shard files (need at least threshold count)

newRelays.description: Relay URLs to configure after recovery

tools.name: identity-restore
tools.description: Re-sign migratable events (profile, contacts, relay list) under the active identity. Skips attestations (trust chain protection).

pubkeyHex.description: Hex pubkey of the original identity to restore from

tools.name: identity-restore-shamir
tools.description: Reconstruct a secret from Shamir shard files. Returns the restored npub for verification.

tools.name: identity-setup
tools.description: Set up a multi-persona identity from the current master secret. Derives named persona sub-identities, optionally creates a Shamir backup and configures relays. Preview mode (confirm: false) shows what would be created without any side effects.

shares.description: Total number of shares to create

relays.description: Relay URLs to configure for all personas

confirm.description: Set true to execute (creates shard files and configures relays); false (default) for preview only

tools.name: identity-switch
tools.description: Switch the active Nostr identity. ALL subsequent tool calls will sign events and query relays as the new identity. Pass "master" to return to root. Returns { npub } of the now-active identity. This is the key tool for multi-persona workflows.

target.description: Purpose, persona name, or "master" to switch to

tools.name: key-decrypt
tools.description: Decrypt an ncryptsec (NIP-49) with a password. Returns the derived pubkey for verification — never the raw key.

tools.name: key-encrypt
tools.description: Encrypt a secret key with a password (NIP-49 ncryptsec). Returns the ncryptsec string and the derived pubkey. WARNING: secret key is transmitted through the MCP transport.

tools.name: key-public
tools.description: Derive a public key (hex + npub) from a secret key (nsec or hex). WARNING: the secret key is transmitted through the MCP transport — use only for local/trusted setups.

secret.description: Secret key as nsec or hex

tools.name: label-create
tools.description: Label an event, pubkey, or addressable event using NIP-32 (kind 1985). Requires a namespace (L tag) and label value (l tag). Provide at least one target: targetEventId (e-tag), targetPubkey (p-tag), or targetAddress (a-tag).

targetAddress.description: Addressable event coordinate to label (a-tag, e.g. "30023:pubkey:slug")

content.description: Optional content for the label event

tools.name: label-read
tools.description: Query NIP-32 labels for a target event, pubkey, or address. Optionally filter by namespace or labeller pubkey.

targetEventId.description: Event ID to look up labels for

targetPubkey.description: Pubkey to look up labels for

targetAddress.description: Addressable event coordinate to look up labels for

tools.name: label-remove
tools.description: Delete a label event via kind 5 deletion. Only works for labels you authored.

labelEventId.description: Event ID of the label to delete

tools.name: label-search
tools.description: Find all events/pubkeys that have been given a specific label in a namespace. Returns label events matching the namespace + value pair.

tools.name: label-self
tools.description: Self-label your own content (kind 1985). Useful for content warnings, categories, or classification. The event must be one you authored.

tools.name: list-bookmark
tools.description: Manage bookmarks (NIP-51). Without a name, manages general bookmarks (kind 10003). With a name, manages a named bookmark set (kind 30001). Supports event IDs, addressable event coordinates, URLs, and hashtags.

name.description: Bookmark set name (omit for general bookmarks)

action.description: Whether to add or remove bookmarks

tools.name: list-bookmark-read
tools.description: Read bookmarks. Without a name, reads general bookmarks (kind 10003). With a name, reads a named bookmark set (kind 30001).

name.description: Bookmark set name (omit for general bookmarks)

tools.name: list-check-muted
tools.description: Check if a pubkey, event ID, keyword, or hashtag is on your mute list. Returns whether the target is muted and what matched.

tools.name: list-followset-create
tools.description: Create a named follow set (NIP-51, kind 30000). Group contacts into named lists (e.g. "developers", "local-community").

pubkeys.description: Initial members — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: list-followset-manage
tools.description: Add or remove pubkeys from a named follow set (NIP-51, kind 30000).

action.description: Whether to add or remove members

pubkeys.description: Members — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: list-followset-read
tools.description: Read a named follow set by name (NIP-51, kind 30000).

tools.name: list-mute
tools.description: Manage your mute list (NIP-51, kind 10000). Add or remove pubkeys, event IDs, keywords, or hashtags. Publishes the updated replaceable event.

action.description: Whether to add or remove entries

value.description: Entry value (hex pubkey, hex event ID, keyword string, or hashtag)

tools.name: list-mute-read
tools.description: Read your current mute list (NIP-51, kind 10000). Returns all muted pubkeys, events, keywords, and hashtags.

tools.name: list-pin
tools.description: Manage pinned events (NIP-51, kind 10001). Add or remove event IDs from your pin list.

action.description: Whether to add or remove pins

eventIds.description: Event IDs to pin or unpin

tools.name: list-pin-read
tools.description: Read your pinned events list (NIP-51, kind 10001).

tools.name: listing-close
tools.description: Mark a classified listing as sold or closed (NIP-99, kind 30402). Fetches the existing listing, preserves all tags, and republishes with a status tag.

tools.name: listing-create
tools.description: Create a classified listing (NIP-99, kind 30402). Publishes a replaceable event with title, description, price, and optional location/hashtags. The slug (d-tag) defaults to a slugified title.

frequency.description: Pricing frequency (e.g. "per hour", "per month") — omit for one-off

slug.description: Custom d-tag identifier — defaults to slugified title

tools.name: listing-read
tools.description: Read classified listing(s) (NIP-99, kind 30402) by author and optional slug. Accepts any identifier for author: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

author.description: Author — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

slug.description: Listing slug (d-tag) — omit to fetch all listings by author

tools.name: listing-search
tools.description: Search classified listings (NIP-99, kind 30402) by hashtag or geohash location. Provide at least one of hashtag or geohash.

tools.name: marketplace-announce
tools.description: Publish a kind 31402 service announcement on Nostr. Advertises your API with pricing, capabilities, and payment methods so other agents can discover and pay for it.

capability.description: Capability name this price applies to

paymentMethods.description: Payment method tuples — first element is the rail (l402, x402, cashu, xcashu, payment), rest are params

tools.name: marketplace-call
tools.description: Make an authenticated API call using L402 credentials obtained from marketplace-pay. The credential ID maps to a stored macaroon + preimage — never exposed directly.

tools.name: marketplace-compare
tools.description: Compare two or more L402 services side by side — pricing, capabilities, and shared features. Provide event IDs; the tool fetches and compares them.

eventIds.description: Event IDs of kind 31402 services to compare

tools.name: marketplace-credentials-clear
tools.description: Clear all stored L402 credentials from memory. Use when switching identity or cleaning up.

tools.name: marketplace-discover
tools.description: Discover L402/x402 paid API services announced on Nostr (kind 31402). Filter by topic, payment method, provider, or maximum price.

tools.name: marketplace-inspect
tools.description: Get full details of a specific L402 service by event ID, or by provider pubkey + identifier.

eventId.description: Event ID of the kind 31402 announcement

pubkey.description: Provider — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (requires identifier)

tools.name: marketplace-pay
tools.description: Pay an L402 invoice via NWC and store credentials for authenticated API calls. SPENDS REAL SATS. Decodes the invoice first — set confirm: true to execute payment. Returns an opaque credential ID for use with marketplace-call.

invoice.description: Bolt11 Lightning invoice from L402 challenge

confirm.description: Set true to execute payment (preview by default)

tools.name: marketplace-probe
tools.description: Send an HTTP request to an endpoint and inspect the response. If the server returns 402, the L402 challenge (macaroon + invoice) is extracted and the cost in sats is estimated.

tools.name: marketplace-reputation
tools.description: Check a service provider's reputation — number of active services, announcement history, and topics covered.

pubkey.description: Provider to check — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: marketplace-retire
tools.description: Retire (delete) a service announcement by publishing a kind 5 deletion event. The service will no longer appear in discovery results.

tools.name: marketplace-search
tools.description: Full-text search across L402 service names, descriptions, topics, and capabilities.

tools.name: marketplace-update
tools.description: Update an existing kind 31402 service announcement. Same pubkey + identifier replaces the previous version (NIP-33 replaceable).

tools.name: moderation-filter
tools.description: Filter a set of events against the active identity's mute list. Returns allowed events and blocked events with reasons. Checks pubkeys, event IDs, keywords (in content), and hashtags (in t-tags).

tools.name: nip-list
tools.description: List all official Nostr NIPs from the protocol repository.

tools.name: nip-publish
tools.description: Publish a community NIP (kind 30817) — a custom protocol specification on Nostr.

kinds.description: Event kinds defined by this NIP

tools.name: nip-read
tools.description: Fetch community NIPs (kind 30817) from relays. Filter by author, identifier, or defined kind.

kind.description: Event kind defined by the NIP

tools.name: nip-show
tools.description: Fetch and display the full content of an official NIP by number.

tools.name: nip05-lookup
tools.description: Resolve a NIP-05 identifier (user@domain) to a Nostr pubkey. Also returns relay hints if the server provides them. Use this to find someone's pubkey from their human-readable address.

tools.name: nip05-relays
tools.description: Fetch relay hints from a NIP-05 identifier. The NIP-05 server can suggest preferred relays for each pubkey. Returns a map of pubkey to relay URLs. Useful for relay discovery before messaging someone.

tools.name: nip05-verify
tools.description: Verify that a NIP-05 identifier (user@domain) resolves to the expected pubkey. Returns { verified: true/false }. Use this to confirm someone's claimed NIP-05 identity.

tools.name: nip44-decrypt
tools.description: Decrypt a NIP-44 ciphertext using the active identity's private key.

senderPubkeyHex.description: Sender — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: nip44-encrypt
tools.description: Encrypt a plaintext string using NIP-44 for a recipient pubkey. Uses the active identity's private key.

recipientPubkeyHex.description: Recipient — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: onboard-verified
tools.description: Guided workflow to build a verified trust profile. Shows your current Signet tier, what steps remain (self-declaration, vouches, professional verification, vault setup), and lists contacts who could vouch for you.

tools.name: post-queue-cancel
tools.description: Cancel a scheduled post by event ID. Removes it from the queue.

tools.name: post-queue-list
tools.description: List all scheduled posts waiting to be published.

tools.name: post-schedule
tools.description: Schedule a Nostr event for future publication. Signs the event now and queues it on disk. Use nostr-bray publish-scheduled (via cron) to publish when due.

content.description: Event content (note text, article body, etc.)

scheduled_at.description: When to publish -- ISO datetime (e.g. "2026-04-01T14:00:00Z") or Unix timestamp

kind.description: Event kind (1 for note, 30023 for article, etc.)

tools.name: privacy-commit
tools.description: Create a Pedersen commitment to a secret value. Returns a public commitment point (safe to share) and a blinding factor (keep secret). The commitment cryptographically binds you to the value without revealing it.

value.description: The secret value to commit to (non-negative integer)

tools.name: privacy-open
tools.description: Verify a Pedersen commitment opening. Given the original value and blinding factor, check they match the public commitment. Returns true if the commitment is valid.

tools.name: privacy-prove-age
tools.description: Prove age is within a range without revealing the exact age. Supports formats like "18+" (adult), "13-17" (teen), "8-12" (child). Optionally bind to a subject pubkey to prevent transplanting.

ageRange.description: Age range to prove: "18+" for adult, "min-max" for band (e.g. "8-12", "13-17")

subjectPubkey.description: Subject hex pubkey to bind the proof to (prevents transplanting)

tools.name: privacy-prove-range
tools.description: Prove a secret value is within [min, max] without revealing the value. Returns a cryptographic range proof that anyone can verify against the commitment. Optional binding context prevents proof transplanting between credentials.

value.description: The secret value to prove is in range

context.description: Optional binding context (e.g. subject pubkey) to prevent proof transplanting

tools.name: privacy-prove-threshold
tools.description: Prove a value exceeds a threshold without revealing the exact value. Useful for income verification, balance checks, credit scoring. The proof shows value >= threshold.

threshold.description: The minimum threshold to prove against

context.description: Optional binding context to prevent proof transplanting

tools.name: privacy-publish-proof
tools.description: Publish a range proof as a kind 30078 (NIP-78) Nostr event. Includes the commitment, proof, range description, and optional subject pubkey. The proof can then be discovered and verified by anyone.

proof.description: The serialised range proof (JSON string) to publish

subjectPubkey.description: Subject hex pubkey — tagged with p-tag for discoverability

tools.name: privacy-read-proof
tools.description: Fetch and verify range proof events from relays. Returns a list of proofs with their verification status, labels, ranges, and subject pubkeys.

tools.name: privacy-verify-age
tools.description: Verify an age range proof. Checks that the subject is within the expected age range without learning their exact age.

proof.description: The serialised age range proof (JSON string)

ageRange.description: Expected age range: "18+" for adult, "min-max" for band

subjectPubkey.description: Expected subject hex pubkey (must match what prover used)

tools.name: privacy-verify-range
tools.description: Verify a range proof. Checks that the committed value is within [min, max] without learning the value. Supply the same binding context used during proof creation.

context.description: Expected binding context (must match what prover used)

tools.name: privacy-verify-threshold
tools.description: Verify a threshold proof. Checks that the committed value is >= threshold without learning the value.

context.description: Expected binding context (must match what prover used)

tools.name: profile-by-name
tools.description: Look up a contact's full profile by name — no pubkey needed. Searches your contacts and returns the kind 0 profile for the match.

name.description: Contact name to search for (case insensitive)

tools.name: publish-event
tools.description: Sign and publish a Nostr event with any kind, content, and tags. Use for custom or experimental event kinds not covered by dedicated tools.

tools.name: relay-add
tools.description: Add a single relay to the active identity's relay set (in-memory only — does not publish kind 10002).

tools.name: relay-auth
tools.description: Authenticate to a relay that requires NIP-42 AUTH. Connects to the relay, waits for an AUTH challenge, signs a kind 22242 event, and sends it back. Returns { authenticated: true/false }. Use this when a relay-query fails due to AUTH requirements, then retry the query.

relay.description: Relay WebSocket URL to authenticate with

tools.name: relay-compare
tools.description: Compare 2 or more relays side-by-side. For each relay: NIP-11 metadata, response time, supported NIPs, whether you have events on it, software, and limitations.

tools.name: relay-count
tools.description: Count events matching a filter without fetching them (NIP-45). Sends a COUNT request to each relay. Falls back to fetch-and-count (capped at 1000) if the relay does not support NIP-45. Results show per-relay counts with fallback/estimated flags. Filter fields may be supplied either as top-level arguments or wrapped in a single "filter" object (both shapes are accepted).

limit.description: Maximum number of events to count per relay

filter.description: Alias: all filter fields wrapped in a single object. Merged with any top-level fields; top-level values win on conflict.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to count per relay

tools.name: relay-discover
tools.description: Discover relays used by your contacts. Fetches kind 10002 (NIP-65) relay lists from your follow list, aggregates relay URLs, and ranks by popularity. Shows which relays your contacts cluster on.

tools.name: relay-diversity
tools.description: Analyse your relay set for centralisation risk. Checks unique operators, software diversity, and reachability. Flags if >50% of relays share an operator. Returns warnings and recommendations.

tools.name: relay-health
tools.description: Check health of the active identity's configured relays. Reports NIP-11 metadata, response time, whether the user has events on the relay, and optionally write access (publishes a short-lived test event).

pubkey.description: Hex pubkey to check for events (defaults to active identity)

checkWrite.description: Publish a test kind 30078 event to verify write access (default false)

tools.name: relay-info
tools.description: Fetch the NIP-11 relay information document for a relay URL.

tools.name: relay-list
tools.description: List the relay set (read/write) for the active identity. Optionally check for shared relays with another identity.

compareWithNpub.description: Compare shared relays with this npub

tools.name: relay-nip-search
tools.description: Find relays that support specific NIPs. Queries NIP-11 info documents and filters to relays supporting requested NIP numbers. Useful for finding relays with search (NIP-50), auth (NIP-42), event counts (NIP-45), or other capabilities.

candidateRelays.description: Relay URLs to check (defaults to your configured relays)

tools.name: relay-query
tools.description: Query events from Nostr relays by kind, author, ids, tags, or time range. Useful for discovering events, scanning for specific kinds, or investigating unknown event schemas. Uses explicit relays if provided, otherwise the active identity's read relays. Filter fields may be supplied either as top-level arguments or wrapped in a single "filter" object (both shapes are accepted).

since.description: Unix timestamp, only events created after this time

until.description: Unix timestamp, only events created before this time

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return (default 50, max 500)

relays.description: Explicit relay URLs to query (overrides identity relay set)

search.description: Full-text search query (NIP-50). Only works on relays that support NIP-50; others will ignore it.

filter.description: Alias: all filter fields wrapped in a single object. Merged with any top-level fields; top-level values win on conflict.

tools.name: relay-recommend
tools.description: Recommend relays for your use case. Uses contact relay discovery, NIP-11 metadata, and health checks to score and rank relay candidates. Strategies: balanced (mix of popular + diverse), privacy (Tor-friendly, no auth), performance (lowest latency), social (relays your contacts cluster on).

tools.name: relay-set
tools.description: Publish a kind 10002 relay list for the active identity. Warns if a relay list already exists.

confirm.description: Set true to overwrite existing relay list

tools.name: safety-activate
tools.description: Switch to an alternative identity configuration.

tools.name: safety-configure
tools.description: Configure an alternative identity persona and pre-warm relay connections.

personaName.description: Persona name for the alternative identity

tools.name: search-notes
tools.description: Full-text search for notes (kind 1) using NIP-50. Requires relay support for NIP-50 — if results are empty, try relay-info to check NIP support. Optionally specify relays known to support NIP-50.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only notes after this time

relays.description: Explicit relay URLs to search (use relays known to support NIP-50)

tools.name: search-profiles
tools.description: Search for Nostr profiles (kind 0) by keyword using NIP-50. Requires relay support for NIP-50 — if results are empty, try relay-info to check NIP support.

tools.name: signet-badge
tools.description: Get a quick trust summary for a pubkey — returns their Signet tier, trust score, composite level, and flags. Use this as a "who is this?" check before interacting with an unknown user.

pubkey.description: User to assess — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: signet-challenge
tools.description: Publish a kind 31000 challenge event against a verifier suspected of misconduct. Provide a reason code and optional supporting evidence text. Use "anomalous-volume" for suspicious attestation rates, "registry-mismatch" when their claimed registration cannot be confirmed, "fraudulent-attestation" for clearly false credentials, "licence-revoked" if their professional licence has lapsed, or "other" for anything else.

verifierPubkey.description: Verifier being challenged — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: signet-credentials
tools.description: List all kind 31000 Signet credentials held by a pubkey — includes tier, type, method, profession, jurisdiction, age range, and expiry. Use this to inspect a user's full credential set.

pubkey.description: User whose credentials to fetch — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: signet-policy-check
tools.description: Check whether a pubkey satisfies a community's Signet access policy. Returns allowed/denied with the required and actual tier/score. Use this before granting access to age-gated or trust-gated content.

pubkey.description: User to check — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

communityId.description: Community identifier (matches the d-tag in the policy event)

tools.name: signet-policy-set
tools.description: Publish a kind 30078 Signet community policy signed by the active identity. Sets minimum verification tiers for adult and child access, optional minimum trust score, and enforcement mode. Use "client" for app-level gating, "relay" to hint relays, "both" for belt-and-braces enforcement.

adultMinTier.description: Minimum Signet tier for adult access (1–4)

childMinTier.description: Minimum Signet tier for under-18 access (1–4)

enforcement.description: Enforcement level: client, relay, or both

tools.name: signet-verifiers
tools.description: Search for Signet-registered professional verifiers. Filter by jurisdiction (e.g. "GB") or profession (e.g. "solicitor"). Returns pubkey, npub, profession, jurisdiction, and professional body. Use this to find a suitable verifier before requesting a credential.

tools.name: signet-vouch
tools.description: Vouch for another user by publishing a kind 31000 attestation signed by the active identity. Use "in-person" when you have physically met the person; "online" for remote verification only. An optional comment can provide context for other verifiers.

pubkey.description: User to vouch for — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

comment.description: Optional context or notes about this vouch

tools.name: social-delete
tools.description: Request deletion of an event you published (kind 5). Relays may or may not honour the request.

tools.name: social-feed
tools.description: Fetch the kind 1 text note feed. Optionally filter by authors.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only fetch posts after this time

trust.description: Trust filter mode: strict (hide untrusted), annotate (show scores), off (no filtering)

tools.name: social-notifications
tools.description: Fetch notifications for the active identity — mentions, replies, reactions, and zap receipts.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only fetch notifications after this time

trust.description: Trust filter mode: strict (hide untrusted), annotate (show scores), off (no filtering)

tools.name: social-post
tools.description: Post a text note (kind 1) signed by the active identity and publish to relays. Returns { id, pubkey, publish: { success, allAccepted, accepted, rejected } } where success is the majority-quorum signal (true when at least one relay accepted and at least half of attempted relays accepted) and allAccepted is true only when every attempted relay accepted. The most common social action.

tools.name: social-profile-get
tools.description: Fetch the kind 0 profile for a Nostr identity. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

pubkey.description: Identity to look up — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: social-profile-set
tools.description: Set the kind 0 profile for the active identity. Warns if profile already exists — set confirm: true to overwrite. Profile fields may be supplied either as top-level arguments or wrapped in a single "profile" object (both shapes are accepted).

profile.description: Alias: all profile fields wrapped in a single object. Merged with any top-level fields; top-level values win on conflict.

confirm.description: Set true to overwrite existing profile

tools.name: social-react
tools.description: React to a Nostr event (kind 7). Pass "+" for like, or any emoji (🤙, ❤️, 🔥). You need the event ID and author pubkey.

tools.name: social-reply
tools.description: Reply to a Nostr event (kind 1) with correct threading tags. You need the event ID and the author's pubkey — get these from social_feed or social_notifications.

relay.description: Relay URL where the parent event was seen

tools.name: social-repost
tools.description: Repost/boost a Nostr event (kind 6) as the active identity.

relay.description: Relay URL where the event was seen

tools.name: tombstone
tools.description: Overwrite an addressable event (kind 30000-39999) with empty content, effectively deleting it from relays. Use this to clean up broken or stale replaceable events.

dTag.description: The d-tag value of the event to tombstone

tools.name: trust-attest
tools.description: Verify someone else's claim by attesting to their assertion event. The subject publishes their claim as a Nostr event; you reference it and say "I verify this." The type is inherited from the referenced event. This is the recommended pattern — it puts the individual at the centre.

assertionId.description: Event ID of the subject's assertion to verify

subject.description: Subject — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (auto-detected from assertion if omitted)

type.description: Explicit type override (usually inherited from the assertion)

summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was verified

assertionRelay.description: Relay hint for the assertion event

tools.name: trust-attest-chain
tools.description: Follow endorsement references to build a transitive trust chain. Starting from a subject, queries attestations about that subject, then follows each attestor as a new subject up to maxDepth. Returns the full chain with validity status for each link.

startSubject.description: Initial subject to trace from — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

type.description: Filter by attestation type (narrows the chain)

maxDepth.description: Maximum chain depth to traverse (default 3, max 10)

tools.name: trust-attest-check-revoked
tools.description: Check if a specific attestation has been revoked. Queries relays for the latest version of the attestation and checks its revocation status. Provide either (type + identifier) for typed attestations or (assertionId | assertionAddress) for assertion-only attestations.

assertionId.description: Event ID of the referenced assertion (for assertion-only attestations)

assertionAddress.description: Addressable coordinate of the referenced assertion

tools.name: trust-attest-filter
tools.description: Build a Nostr relay filter for attestation queries. Returns a filter object suitable for relay subscriptions. Supports filtering by type, subject, attestor, schema, and time range.

subject.description: Subject — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

attestor.description: Attestor — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

since.description: Unix timestamp — only events after this time

until.description: Unix timestamp — only events before this time

tools.name: trust-attest-parse
tools.description: Parse a kind 31000 attestation event into a fully typed object with all metadata fields: type, subject, assertion references, temporal fields (occurredAt, validFrom, validTo), expiration, schema, revocation status, and content.

event.description: The attestation event object to parse

tools.name: trust-attest-temporal
tools.description: Create and publish an attestation with occurredAt field — records when the attested event actually happened, which may differ from the publication time. Also supports validFrom/validTo for deferred activation and validity windows.

subject.description: Subject — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

assertionId.description: Event ID of the assertion to verify

assertionRelay.description: Relay hint for the assertion event

occurredAt.description: Unix timestamp when the attested event actually occurred

validTo.description: Unix timestamp for validity window end

tools.name: trust-claim
tools.description: Make a direct statement about another identity — an endorsement, review, vouch, or any attestor-originated claim. You define the type and subject. Use trust-attest instead if the subject has published their own assertion event.

subject.description: Subject — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (omit for self-declarations)

assertionAddress.description: Addressable event coordinate kind:pubkey:d-tag (produces an a-tag with "assertion" marker, e.g. for attesting authorship of a kind 30817 community NIP)

assertionRelay.description: Relay hint for the assertion address

tools.name: trust-proof-publish
tools.description: Publish a cryptographic linkage proof as a kind 30078 event. Requires confirmation — this is irreversible.

mode.description: Proof mode: blind hides derivation path, full reveals it

tools.name: trust-read
tools.description: Read kind 31000 attestations from relays. Filter by subject, type, or attestor. Works with both assertion-first (trust-attest) and direct claims (trust-claim).

subject.description: Subject — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

attestor.description: Attestor — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: trust-request
tools.description: Send an attestation request to another Nostr identity via NIP-17 encrypted DM.

recipientPubkeyHex.description: Attestor to request from — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

subject.description: Subject to be attested — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: trust-request-list
tools.description: Scan received NIP-17 DMs for attestation request payloads.

tools.name: trust-revoke
tools.description: Revoke a previously issued attestation. Active identity must match the original attestor.

originalAttestorPubkey.description: Hex pubkey of the original attestor (for verification)

tools.name: trust-ring-key-image
tools.description: Compute the key image for the active identity in a specific election context. Use this to pre-check whether the active identity has already signed in an election without creating a full signature. The key image is deterministic: same key + same electionId always produces the same image.

tools.name: trust-ring-lsag-sign
tools.description: Create an LSAG (Linkable SAG) signature with a key image tied to an election ID. If the same signer signs twice in the same election, the duplicate key image reveals double-action without revealing identity. Use for anonymous voting, one-per-person actions, or fair resource allocation.

ring.description: Ring members — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (must include active identity)

electionId.description: Election/context identifier — key image is bound to this (e.g. "vote-2026-q1")

tools.name: trust-ring-lsag-verify
tools.description: Verify an LSAG signature and check the key image against a list of known images to detect double-signing. Returns validity, key image, and duplicate status.

signature.description: LSAG signature object or Nostr event containing one

existingKeyImages.description: Known key images to check for duplicates

tools.name: trust-ring-prove
tools.description: Create a ring signature proving anonymous group membership. A verifier can confirm "someone in this ring signed this" but cannot determine who. The active identity must be one of the pubkeys in the ring. Returns kind 30078 event with the signature.

ring.description: Ring members — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (must include active identity)

attestationType.description: Attestation type context for the canonical message

message.description: Custom message to sign (defaults to canonical format)

tools.name: trust-ring-verify
tools.description: Verify a ring signature proof.

signature.description: Ring signature object or Nostr event containing one

tools.name: trust-score
tools.description: Compute a trust score for a Nostr identity using the web-of-trust graph and kind 31000 attestations. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

pubkey.description: Identity to score — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: trust-spoken-challenge
tools.description: Generate a spoken verification token for in-person identity confirmation.

tools.name: trust-spoken-directional
tools.description: Generate a directional token pair where each role gets a different token. Prevents the "echo problem" where the second speaker could parrot the first. Both parties need the shared secret, but each says a different word. Ideal for caller/agent, buyer/seller, or any two-party verification.

wordCount.description: Number of words (for words format, default 1)

pinDigits.description: Number of digits (for PIN format, default 4)

hexLength.description: Number of hex chars (for hex format, default 8)

tools.name: trust-spoken-encode
tools.description: Generate a spoken token in an alternative encoding: PIN digits for phone keypads, hex for technical contexts, or multi-word for higher entropy. Same HMAC derivation as trust-spoken-challenge but with configurable output format.

wordCount.description: Number of words (for words format, default 1)

pinDigits.description: Number of digits (for PIN format, default 4)

hexLength.description: Number of hex chars (for hex format, default 8)

tools.name: trust-spoken-verify
tools.description: Verify a spoken token response against the shared secret.

tools.name: trust-verify
tools.description: Validate the structural correctness of a kind 31000 attestation event.

event.description: The attestation event object to validate

tools.name: vault-config
tools.description: Fetch a vault config and return a summary of tier names, member counts, revoked count, grant count, and current epoch. Optionally inspect another author's vault.

authorPubkey.description: Vault owner to inspect — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (defaults to active identity)

tools.name: vault-create
tools.description: Create a Dominion vault config with named access tiers. Signs and publishes the config as a kind 30078 event. Returns the created event, publish result, and tier names.

tiers.description: Names of the access tiers to create (e.g. ["admin", "member", "guest"])

tools.name: vault-encrypt
tools.description: Encrypt content using a content key derived from the active identity for a given tier and epoch. No network access — purely local cryptography. Returns the ciphertext, tier, and epoch used.

tier.description: Access tier name to derive the content key for

epoch.description: Epoch ID to use (defaults to current epoch if omitted)

tools.name: vault-members
tools.description: Fetch a vault config and list all members annotated with trust levels. Optionally query another author's vault by passing their pubkey. Returns members with npub, tier, and trust annotation.

authorPubkey.description: Vault owner to inspect — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (defaults to active identity)

tools.name: vault-read
tools.description: Decrypt ciphertext using the content key derived from the active identity for a given tier and epoch. No network access — purely local cryptography. Returns the plaintext, tier, and epoch.

tier.description: Access tier name the content was encrypted for

epoch.description: Epoch ID that was used when encrypting

tools.name: vault-read-shared
tools.description: Decrypt ciphertext using a vault key that was shared with you by another identity. Fetches the encrypted share event from relays, decrypts the content key via NIP-44, then decrypts the ciphertext. Use this when you are a recipient of vault-share, not the vault owner.

authorPubkey.description: Identity that shared the vault key — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tier.description: Access tier name the content was encrypted for

epoch.description: Epoch ID that was used when encrypting

tools.name: vault-revoke
tools.description: Revoke a pubkey from the vault config. Fetches the current config, adds the pubkey to the revoked list, signs, and republishes the updated config event. Returns the updated event and revoked npub.

pubkey.description: Identity to revoke — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

tools.name: vault-rotate
tools.description: Show the current epoch ID and explain how epoch-based key rotation works. Purely informational — to rotate access, revoke recipients and re-share keys for the next epoch.

tools.name: vault-share
tools.description: Derive the content key for a tier and epoch, then distribute encrypted vault shares to recipients via gift-wrapped events. Returns counts of published and failed shares.

tier.description: Access tier name to share the content key for

recipients.description: Recipients — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey for each

epoch.description: Epoch ID to share (defaults to current epoch if omitted)

tools.name: verify-event
tools.description: Verify a Nostr event's id hash and cryptographic signature.

event.description: The Nostr event object to verify

tools.name: verify-person
tools.description: Verify a Nostr identity: checks NIP-05, trust score, kind 31000 attestations, linkage proofs, and (in full mode) ring endorsements and a spoken challenge token. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

pubkey.description: Identity to verify — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey

method.description: Verification depth — quick (default) or full (includes ring proofs and spoken challenge)

tools.name: whoami
tools.description: Returns the active identity's npub. The simplest way to check which identity is currently in use.

tools.name: wiki-list
tools.description: List wiki topics (NIP-54, kind 30818). Returns unique topics with latest title, summary, and author. Optionally filter by author. Accepts any identifier: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

author.description: Author — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (omit to list all topics)

tools.name: wiki-publish
tools.description: Publish or update a wiki article (NIP-54, kind 30818). Creates a replaceable event keyed by topic (d-tag). Content convention is Asciidoc but Markdown is widely accepted. Articles are collaborative — anyone can publish a revision for the same topic.

topic.description: Article topic / slug (d-tag) — lowercase, hyphens instead of spaces

tools.name: wiki-read
tools.description: Read wiki article(s) by topic (NIP-54, kind 30818). Without an author, returns versions from multiple authors sorted by recency — readers pick the version from the author they trust most. Accepts any identifier for author: name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey.

author.description: Author — name, NIP-05, npub, or hex pubkey (omit to see all authors)

limit.description: Max articles to return (when no author specified)

tools.name: zap-balance
tools.description: Request wallet balance via NWC. Sends a get_balance request to the wallet service.

tools.name: zap-decode
tools.description: Decode basic fields from a bolt11 Lightning invoice string.

tools.name: zap-list-transactions
tools.description: List recent Lightning transactions via NWC.

tools.name: zap-lookup-invoice
tools.description: Look up a Lightning invoice status via NWC.

tools.name: zap-make-invoice
tools.description: Generate a Lightning invoice via NWC to receive payments.

tools.name: zap-receipts
tools.description: Fetch zap receipts (kind 9735) for the active identity. Returns sender, amount in msats, and message.

since.description: Unix timestamp — only fetch zaps after this time

tools.name: zap-send
tools.description: Pay a Lightning invoice via Nostr Wallet Connect (NWC). SPENDS REAL SATS. Decodes the invoice and shows amount first — set confirm: true to execute payment.

confirm.description: Set true to execute payment (preview by default)

tools.name: zap-wallet-clear
tools.description: Remove the NWC wallet for the active identity. Falls back to the global NWC_URI if set.

tools.name: zap-wallet-set
tools.description: Set the NWC wallet URI for the active identity. Each persona can have its own Lightning wallet.
